data.body:
### [8/10] DOSSIER C — Binding lifecycle & identity: WHY (each rule = a production incident)

The binding store maps local_id<->jira_key and is the AUTHORITATIVE synced-relationship set. Lessons L9-L17 + c244, each incident-forced:
- L9 (rebar_id_audit.py:66-90, story 4496): unaudited rebar-id label writes minted ghost/duplicate identities -> ONLY 3 of 9 dispatch leaves may write a rebar-id label (outbound_create+inbound_create add, inbound_clean_label delete); every write audited. Identity is unrepairable after the fact.
- L10 (bug 4354, differ.py:457-504): a bound issue in curr-not-prev was mis-classified unbound-new -> minted a phantom jira-dig-NNNN local AND wrote a ghost rebar-id:jira-dig-5029 label back to Jira (observed 2026-05-29 DIG-5029). Fix: label-derived-binding suppression — a rebar-id-labeled snapshot key stands the snapshot differ down.
- L11 (story 7a75, invariants.py:234-307): double-bind (two Jira issues claim one local_id) -> quarantine offenders, capped per pass (can't know which claimant is legit; auto-repair risks destroying the real link).
- L13 (bug 1e08, outbound_differ.py): a bound key absent from the snapshot diffed against {} re-emitted every field every pass. Fix: membership != value; liveness resolved by a bounded, rotated direct GET (200=alive overlay, 404=deleted, else defer). Classifier returns a FOUR-way ObservedJira state (PRESENT|CONFIRMED_404|ABSENT_IN_WINDOW|TRANSPORT_ERROR), never a boolean.
- L14/L15 (bug 1e08, binding_store.py:403-448): retirement requires N CONSECUTIVE confirmed-404s (RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE default 3); a 200 resets; retirement is a REVERSIBLE soft-delete to the SEPARATE bindings-retired.json. ASYMMETRIC failure: bindings.json fails CLOSED (corrupt=>abort, else mass-duplicate); bindings-retired.json fails OPEN (corrupt=>empty+alert; a wrongly-live retired binding costs one wasted GET).
- L16 (bug 0702, outbound_differ.py:623-634): a 200 overlay is shared to inbound (avoids 2nd GET); 404/transport DELIBERATELY excluded so a gone issue is never inbound-mirrored (retirement stays outbound-owned).
- L17 (bug f6cc, fetcher.py:23-94): a single JQL hit the ~1000-issue ACLI ceiling -> Done backlog looked absent -> mass-retire. Fix: split JQL (status!=Done active + status=Done ORDER BY updated capped at _DONE_RECENT_CAP); Done-beyond-cap is alive-out-of-window, NOT deleted. Circuit breaker (refuse a pass mutating >N% of bindings) = defense-in-depth.
- c244 (binding_store.py:325-336, commit cbfb664ec): proven 404 on a still-local ticket => re-create Jira SAME pass (create_after_hard_delete); bind_confirm drops the stale reverse[old_key].
- Write-ahead protocol (binding_store.py:289-336,479-536, story 9622): bind_pending -> create -> record_pending_key -> plant marker -> bind_confirm -> save; recover_pending_bindings re-derives PENDING (never confirmed) from the rebar-id label at startup with NO Jira search.

"AUTHORITATIVE yet a derived cache" reconciled: AUTHORITATIVE operationally (the pass reads it for identity; corruption fails the pass CLOSED, _ids.py:56 "the authoritative Jira<->rebar mapping"); DERIVED by provenance (ground truth = the rebar-id label on the Jira issue [L9] + the local ticket's local_id; bindings.json is a fast, rebuildable, drift-corrected index — recover_pending_bindings, check_dual_identity_complete, bridge_fsck.audit_binding_drift). This is exactly why epic 6c9c DROPPED WS-1 (event-sourcing the data) — the convergence loop already detects+self-heals drift.
WHY ordinary clones read bindings.json (_ids.py:50-75): _resolve_via_binding_store resolves a Jira key -> local ticket dir via the `reverse` index for generic ID resolution (`rebar show REB-310`); can't be reconciler-only because the reconciler runs in CI not in dev/agent clones. Best-effort/never-raises; touches ONLY `reverse` (identity), NEVER the baseline.

BEARING: digest is IDENTITY-SAFE — grep proves `baseline` is read by ZERO of rebar_id_audit.py (L9), invariants.py (L11), differ.py (L10), _ids.py (clone reader). Drift KINDS (audit_binding_drift, census) are baseline-independent (classify.py:148 explicitly does NOT consume baseline for lifecycle routing). The digest's ONLY hazard is ADR 0026 field arbitration ([7]). One-time reclaim: recover_pending_bindings reads the LIVE in-memory store (not history); retirement is the SEPARATE bindings-retired.json — both unaffected. BUT the fail-closed corruption path restores via `git show tickets:.../bindings.json` (binding_store.py:145-146) => the reclaim MUST preserve the last-known-good bindings.json revision + keep the `.bridge_state/* merge=ours` attribute + exclude bindings-retired.json.
